heading: Special appropriations for Mars missions, Artemis missions, and Moon to Mars program
label: 20306 Special appropriations for Mars missions, Artemis missions, and Moon to Mars program
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsecs. (a)(2), (6)(F) and (b)(1), (3), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 119–21July 4, 2025

children.text: which shall be delivered to the Administration not later than .December 31, 2028

$2,600,000,000 to meet the requirements of section 20302(a) using the program of record known, as of the date of the enactment of this section, as “Gateway”, and as described in section 10811(b)(2)(B)(iv) of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration Authorization Act of 2022 ( note; ), of which not less than $750,000,000 shall be obligated for each of fiscal years 2026, 2027, and 2028.51 U.S.C. 20302Public Law 117–167

$4,100,000,000 for expenses related to meeting the requirements of section 10812 of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration Authorization Act of 2022 (; ) for the procurement, transportation, integration, operation, and other necessary expenses of the Space Launch System for Artemis Missions IV and V, of which not less than $1,025,000,000 shall be obligated for each of fiscal years 2026, 2027, 2028, and 2029.51 U.S.C. 203011Public Law 117–167So in original. Probably should be “ note;”.151 U.S.C. 20301

$20,000,000 for expenses related to the continued procurement of the multi-purpose crew vehicle described in section 303 of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration Authorization Act of 2010 (), known as the “Orion”, for use with the Space Launch System on the Artemis IV Mission and reuse in subsequent Artemis Missions, of which not less than $20,000,000 shall be obligated not later than fiscal year 2026.42 U.S.C. 18323

$1,250,000,000 for expenses related to the operation of the International Space Station and for the purpose of meeting the requirement under section 503(a) of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration Authorization Act of 2010 (), of which not less than $250,000,000 shall be obligated for such expenses for each of fiscal years 2025, 2026, 2027, 2028, and 2029.42 U.S.C. 18353(a)

children.label: A
children.text: $120,000,000 shall be obligated not later than fiscal year 2026 for construction, revitalization, recapitalization, or other infrastructure projects and improvements at the center described in Executive Order 12641 (53 Fed. Reg. 18816; relating to designating certain facilities of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration in the State of Mississippi as the John C. Stennis Space Center);

children.label: B
children.text: $250,000,000 shall be obligated not later than fiscal year 2026 for construction, revitalization, recapitalization, or other infrastructure projects and improvements at the center described in Executive Order 11129 (28 Fed. Reg. 12787; relating to designating certain facilities of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration and of the Department of Defense, in the State of Florida, as the John F. Kennedy Space Center);

children.label: C
children.text: $300,000,000 shall be obligated not later than fiscal year 2026 for construction, revitalization, recapitalization, or other infrastructure projects and improvements at the center described in the Joint Resolution entitled “Joint Resolution to designate the Manned Spacecraft Center in Houston, Texas, as the ‘Lyndon B. Johnson Space Center’ in honor of the late President”, approved (; );February 17, 1973Public Law 93–887 Stat. 7

children.label: D

Reg. 2197; relating to designating the facilities of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration at Huntsville, Alabama, as the George C. Marshall Space Flight Center);

children.label: E
children.text: $30,000,000 shall be obligated not later than fiscal year 2026 for construction, revitalization, recapitalization, or other infrastructure projects and improvements at the Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans, Louisiana; and

children.label: F
children.text: $85,000,000 shall be obligated to carry out subsection (b), of which not less than $5,000,000 shall be obligated for the transportation of the space vehicle described in that subsection, with the remainder transferred not later than the date that is 18 months after the date of the enactment of this section to the entity designated under that subsection, for the purpose of construction of a facility to house the space vehicle referred to in that subsection.
$325,000,000 to fulfill contract number 80JSC024CA002 issued by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration on .June 26, 2024

children.heading: .—Transfer
children.label: 3 .—Transfer
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of the enactment of this section, the space vehicle identified under paragraph (1) shall be transferred to an entity designated by the Administrator.
Not less than 50 percent of the total funds in subsection (a) shall be obligated not later than .September 30, 2028
100 percent of funds shall be obligated not later than .September 30, 2029
All associated outlays shall occur not later than .September 30, 2034
